lessons.title: Importing Modules: Using Python's Built-in Libraries

issues.recommendation: Add estimatedDuration to lesson.json (suggested: 15-20 minutes for this topic)

lessons.notes: Good coverage of random module, os module, datetime module. Content appropriate for Python 3.12 target version.

lessons.title: Creating Your Own Modules
lessons.notes: Good coverage of __name__ == '__main__' pattern, module creation, and best practices. Content accurate for Python 3.12.

lessons.title: Packages and Project Structure

issues.description: Warning about __init__.py could be more nuanced for modern Python
issues.details: Content says __init__.py is required to make directory a package. Since Python 3.3+, namespace packages allow directories without __init__.py to be packages, though explicit __init__.py is still best practice for regular packages. Consider adding a note about this for completeness.
issues.recommendation: Add a brief note: 'While Python 3.3+ supports namespace packages without __init__.py, it's still best practice to include an __init__.py for explicit package definition.'

lessons.notes: Good coverage of package structure, __init__.py, relative imports, and nested packages.

lessons.title: Modern Python Tooling with uv

issues.description: Content repeatedly references Python 3.13 instead of target version 3.12
issues.details: Multiple files in this lesson reference Python 3.13 extensively (e.g., 'target-version = py313', 'requires-python = >=3.13', 'Python 3.13 is the current stable version'). However, the module target version is specified as Python 3.12. This is inconsistent with the course-wide target version.
examples: content/01-theory.md: 'Python 3.13 is the current stable version. Python 3.14 was released in October 2025.'
content/03-example.md: 'target-version = "py313"' and multiple references to Python 3.13
issues.recommendation: Update all references from Python 3.13 to Python 3.12 to match the module target version. This includes target-version = 'py312' and requires-python = '>=3.12'

issues.description: Inaccurate statement about Python 3.14 release date
issues.details: Content states 'Python 3.14 was released in October 2025.' This is factually incorrect - Python 3.14 is scheduled for release in October 2026, not 2025. Python 3.13 was released in October 2024.
issues.recommendation: Correct the statement to: 'Python 3.14 is scheduled for release in October 2026.'

issues.description: Could mention PEP 621 as the standard pyproject.toml format
issues.recommendation: Add a brief note referencing PEP 621 as the standard for [project] table in pyproject.toml

lessons.notes: Comprehensive coverage of uv tool. However, the version inconsistencies with Python 3.13 vs 3.12 target need to be addressed. The Python 3.14 release date is also incorrect.

lessons.title: Code Quality with Ruff

issues.description: Content references Python 3.13 instead of target version 3.12
issues.details: Multiple configuration examples show 'target-version = py313' and references to Python 3.13-specific rules. This contradicts the Python 3.12 target version for the module.
issues.recommendation: Update all Ruff target-version from 'py313' to 'py312' to match the module target version. Also update any Python 3.13-specific references.

issues.description: Ruff version v0.8.0 mentioned in pre-commit config may become outdated
issues.details: Content references 'rev: v0.8.0' for ruff-pre-commit. This is a specific version that will become outdated. While not critical, consider mentioning that users should check for the latest version.
issues.recommendation: Add a comment: '# Check https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff-pre-commit for latest version'

lessons.notes: Good comprehensive coverage of Ruff. However, all examples use Python 3.13 target version instead of the module's target 3.12. This needs to be corrected for consistency.

lessons.title: Popular Third-Party Packages

issues.details: lesson.json for L06 only contains id and title fields. Missing: description, difficulty, learningObjectives, prerequisites, estimatedDuration, content, and challenges array.
issues.recommendation: Complete the lesson.json with all required fields following the schema used in other lessons (e.g., L01-L05, L07)

issues.description: Package version numbers will become outdated
issues.details: Content shows specific package versions (requests 2.31.0, pandas 2.0.0, etc.). These will become outdated over time. Consider adding a note about checking for latest versions.
issues.recommendation: Add a note: 'Version numbers shown are examples - always check for the latest stable versions'

lessons.notes: Lesson 06 has an incomplete lesson.json that needs to be completed. Content files exist but the metadata is missing.

lessons.title: Mini-Project: Python Project Initializer

issues.description: Mini-project examples reference Python 3.13 instead of 3.12
issues.details: The mini-project content shows 'target-version = py313' and Python 3.13 references, inconsistent with the 3.12 target.
issues.recommendation: Update to 'py312' for consistency with the module target version

lessons.notes: Good comprehensive mini-project that ties together concepts from the entire module. Content is well-structured.

strengths: Excellent coverage of modern Python tooling with uv and Ruff
Comprehensive mini-project that integrates all module concepts
Good progression from basic imports to advanced tooling
Content is generally well-structured and pedagogically sound
concerns: Major version inconsistency: Content repeatedly references Python 3.13 but module targets 3.12
Incorrect Python 3.14 release date (October 2025 vs actual October 2026)
Multiple lesson.json files missing estimatedDuration field
recommendations: Standardize all Python version references to 3.12 throughout the module
Complete lesson.json for Lesson 06 with all required fields
Correct Python 3.14 release date in Lesson 04
Add estimatedDuration to all lesson.json files
Consider adding a module-level note about checking for latest package/tool versions
